title: The income celebration log
subtitle: A five-minute writing practice for anchoring new money wins into the body and memory.
excerpt: A short activity from Day 49 of the MONEY program. Keep a running log of income wins — not just large ones. The practice teaches the nervous system that money arriving is a normal, safe, repeating event.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 49 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use each time money arrives — a payment notification, a new client, a refund, a small win. Any income counts.

content.text: A short activity from Day 49 of the MONEY program. The nervous system learns from repetition. If income arriving always passes without acknowledgement, the body never fully records it as a safe, normal event. This practice creates a record — not a budget, not a target, just a log of the times money came in.

Get a notebook, a notes app, or a single document. Title the page: "Money that came in." Start with today. You will add to this the same way you would a gratitude list — briefly, regularly, without skipping small amounts.
Each time money arrives, write one line: the date, the amount, the source. Then add one word that describes how it feels to receive it. That last word is the important part.
The amount does not matter. A small royalty and a large invoice both count. The log is training you to notice, not to total.
Once a week, read the last seven entries out loud. Notice the pattern: money arrives. It does not stop. You are someone it comes to.
Adapted from Day 49 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
